title: Cancel pending notifications when an item transitions to completed
summary: Added a synchronous `onItemCompleted: ((String) -> Void)?` hook to TrainStore that fires the predictor key when a session flips to .completed or .modified. Wired the hook in OwloryApp.swift to call ReminderScheduler.cancelReminder(forKey:) at the moment of completion. Enhanced cancelReminder to also remove already-delivered notifications so stale 'window has passed' banners don't linger when the user completes after delivery. Added 3 domain regression tests; one (testCompletingSessionCallsOnItemCompletedHookWithPredictorKey) was confirmed to FAIL on current main before the TrainStore signature change landed (error: extra argument 'onItemCompleted' in call). Now passes.
files_changed: owlory_xcode/Owlory/Core/Application/TrainStore.swift (new onItemCompleted init param + fire-on-completion in updateSession)
owlory_xcode/Owlory/OwloryApp.swift (passes a closure to TrainStore that calls reminderScheduler.cancelReminder; bridged via @MainActor Task)

bug_shape.user_report: an train item would be completed but a notification will appear stating the window has passed.
bug_shape.root_cause: ReminderScheduler.cancelReminder(forKey:) existed but was never called from any store. Completion-aware suppression only ran via the bulk reschedule() rebuild path, which depends on RootTabView.refreshRuntimeArtifacts() firing — which depends on SwiftUI's .onChange of reminderRefreshKey, which doesn't fire reliably when the app is backgrounded or when the notification has already been delivered. Result: a session marked completed in the View flips trainStore.sessions[i].status, the bulk reschedule eventually runs and removes the pending request, but by then the OS may have already delivered the notification, and removePendingNotificationRequests doesn't touch delivered ones.
fix_shape: (1) TrainStore now fires a synchronous onItemCompleted callback at completion time with the CompletionTimePredictor key. This guarantees per-item cancellation happens immediately on the same MainActor turn as the status flip, not on a downstream View observer.
(2) ReminderScheduler.cancelReminder now removes BOTH pending and delivered notifications, so a stale window-passed banner that arrived just before completion is cleared from Notification Center too.
(3) Existing bulk reschedule() suppression preserved as belt-and-suspenders.

not_covered_in_this_slice: HomeStore task completion (no user-reported notification bug yet; the bulk reschedule path still covers the View-foregrounded case)
TodayStore focus-item completion (same; the Focus Three Done swipe path)
scope_limit.rationale: User-reported bug names train items specifically; the fix is shaped to address that path with a tight, testable change. The same closure-injection pattern generalizes to the other stores trivially. Recommended follow-up slice: app-reminders-cancel-pending-on-home-and-today-completion.

validation.make test-domain DOMAIN=train: TEST SUCCEEDED (includes the 3 new regression tests)
validation.regression-test-failed-before-fix: Verified: TrainStoreTests.testCompletingSessionCallsOnItemCompletedHookWithPredictorKey fails with 'extra argument onItemCompleted in call' before TrainStore gains the init param; passes after.

not_claimed: HomeStore or TodayStore completion paths also cancel reminders (they don't in this slice; queued follow-up)
delivered notifications are removed for non-train items (the cancelReminder API now removes delivered for any key, but only TrainStore wires it currently)
background-fetch-time completion (the closure fires only on MainActor; background completion paths are not in scope)
lock-screen / Siri / widget completion (those paths bypass the View; if they call TrainStore directly the hook fires too, but indirect paths via shared persistence haven't been audited)
residual_risk: If a future code path mutates session.status to .completed or .modified without going through TrainStore.updateSession, the hook won't fire. The current code only mutates status via updateSession; if a path bypasses it (e.g., direct repository write), add a regression test that catches that.

The closure runs synchronously on MainActor at completion time. If reminderScheduler is replaced or invalidated at runtime, the captured `scheduler` reference in OwloryApp could outlive the legitimate window. The closure captures the long-lived OwloryApp-scope scheduler so this is not currently a hazard, but future refactors should keep that lifetime in mind.
downstream_recommended: app-reminders-cancel-pending-on-home-and-today-completion: apply the same onItemCompleted callback pattern to HomeStore (task and protocol run completion) and TodayStore (focus-item Done swipe).
